methods.name: soda.contracts.impl.json_schema_verifier.JsonSchemaVerifier.verify
documentation:
Verifies that the YAML data structure matches the data contract schema.
Swallows all errors and exceptions and appends them to self.logs.

methods.name: soda.contracts.contract.Contract.__parse
documentation: Dry run: parse but not verify the contract to get the errors in the logs.

classes.name: soda.contracts.contract.ContractResult

methods.name: soda.contracts.contract.ContractResult.failed
documentation:
Returns true if there are checks that have failed.
Ignores execution errors in the logs.

methods.name: soda.contracts.contract.ContractResult.passed
documentation:
Returns true if there are no checks that have failed.
Ignores execution errors in the logs.

This is the immutable data structure containing all the results from a single contract verification.
This includes any potential execution errors as well as the results of all the checks performed.

classes.name: soda.contracts.impl.contract_data_source.ContractDataSource

methods.name: soda.contracts.impl.contract_data_source.ContractDataSource.open_connection
documentation:
Ensures that an open connection is available.
After this method ends, the open connection must have the database_name and optionally
the schema_name as context as far as these are applicable for the specific data source type.
Potentially closes and re-opens the self.connection if the existing connection has a different database
or schema context compared to the given database_name and schema_name for the next contract.

methods.name: soda.contracts.impl.contract_data_source.ContractDataSource.set_contract_context
documentation:
Ensures that an open connection is available.
After this method ends, the open connection must have the database_name and optionally
the schema_name as context as far as these are applicable for the specific data source type.
Potentially closes and re-opens the self.connection if the existing connection has a different database
or schema context compared to the given database_name and schema_name for the next contract.

methods.name: soda.contracts.impl.contract_data_source.ContractDataSource._create_connection
documentation:
self.connection_yaml_dict is provided as a parameter for convenience.
Returns a new DBAPI connection based on the provided connection properties.
The returned value will be saved in self.connection. See open() for details.
Exceptions do not need to be handled.  They will be handled by the calling method.

methods.name: soda.contracts.impl.contract_data_source.ContractDataSource._create_sodacl_data_source
documentation: Create SodaCL DataSource using the self.connection and sodacl_data_source_name

methods.name: soda.contracts.impl.contract_data_source.ContractDataSource.close_connection
documentation:
Closes te connection. This method will not throw any exceptions.
Check errors with has_errors or assert_no_errors.

Takes configuration as input that is usually loaded from a data source YAML file.
Is responsible for creating the connection in a with-block.  (see __enter__ and __exit__)
Exposes methods to the rest of the engine that encapsulate interaction with the SQL connection.
This class uses self.sql_dialect to build the SQL statement strings.

classes.name: soda.contracts.impl.sql_dialect.SqlDialect

methods.name: soda.contracts.impl.sql_dialect.SqlDialect.stmt_select_table_names
documentation: Builds the full SQL query to query table names from the data source metadata.

methods.name: soda.contracts.impl.sql_dialect.SqlDialect.sql_information_schema_tables_database_column
documentation: Name of the column in the information_schema table that contains the database name

methods.name: soda.contracts.impl.sql_dialect.SqlDialect.sql_information_schema_tables_schema_column
documentation: Name of the column in the information_schema table that contains the schema name

methods.name: soda.contracts.impl.sql_dialect.SqlDialect.sql_information_schema_tables_table_column
documentation: Name of the column in the information_schema table that contains the table name

methods.name: soda.contracts.impl.sql_dialect.SqlDialect._filter_clauses_information_schema_table_name
documentation:
Builds the list of where clauses to query table names from the data source metadata.
All comparisons are case-insensitive by converting to lower case.
All column names are quoted.

methods.name: soda.contracts.impl.sql_dialect.SqlDialect.qualify_table
documentation: Creates a fully qualified table name, optionally quoting the table name

Extends ContractDataSource with all logic to builds the SQL queries.
Specific ContractDataSource's can customize their SQL queries by subclassing SqlDialect,
overriding methods of SqlDialect and returning the customized SqlDialect in ContractDataSource._create_sql_dialect()

classes.name: soda.contracts.impl.yaml_helper.YamlFile

methods.name: soda.contracts.impl.yaml_helper.YamlFile.is_ok
documentation: True if the yaml dict is available and the parsing didn't have errors

methods.name: soda.contracts.impl.yaml_helper.YamlFile.parse
documentation: returns is_ok() indicating that parsing was successful and the dict is available

Allows yaml configurations to be specified as a file_path, yaml_str or a yaml_dict.
If yaml configurations are specified as text (file or str), then parsing will resolve variables.
Then YamlFile provides access to resolved and parsed dict.
Usage:
variables: dict = {...}
yaml_file: YamlFile = YamlFile(logs=logs, ...use 1 of the other arguments to specify the yaml file content...)
yaml_file.parse(variables)
yaml_dict: dict = yaml_file.get_dict()

methods.name: soda.contracts.impl.yaml_helper.YamlHelper.read_dict
documentation:
An error is generated if the value is missing or not a YAML object.
\:return: a dict if the value for the key is a YAML object, otherwise None.

methods.name: soda.contracts.impl.yaml_helper.YamlHelper.read_dict_opt
documentation:
An error is generated if the value is present and not a YAML object.
\:return: a dict if the value for the key is a YAML object, otherwise None.

methods.name: soda.contracts.impl.yaml_helper.YamlHelper.read_list
documentation:
An error is generated if the value is missing or not a YAML list.
\:return: a list if the value for the key is a YAML list, otherwise None.

methods.name: soda.contracts.impl.yaml_helper.YamlHelper.read_list_opt
documentation:
An error is generated if the value is present and not a YAML list.
\:return: a list if the value for the key is a YAML list, otherwise None.

methods.name: soda.contracts.impl.yaml_helper.YamlHelper.read_string
documentation:
An error is generated if the value is missing or not a string.
\:return: a str if the value for the key is a YAML string, otherwise None.

methods.name: soda.contracts.impl.yaml_helper.YamlHelper.read_string_opt
documentation:
An error is generated if the value is present and not a string.
\:return: a str if the value for the key is a string, otherwise None.

methods.name: soda.contracts.impl.yaml_helper.YamlHelper.read_bool
documentation:
An appropriate error log is generated if the value is not a bool or if the key is missing
\:return: a bool if the value for the key is a YAML boolean, otherwise None.

methods.name: soda.contracts.impl.yaml_helper.YamlHelper.read_bool_opt
documentation:
An appropriate error log is generated if the value is not a bool.
\:return: a bool if the value for the key is a YAML boolean, otherwise None.

methods.name: soda.contracts.impl.yaml_helper.YamlHelper.read_number
documentation:
An appropriate error log is generated if the value is not a number or if the key is missing
\:return: a bool if the value for the key is a YAML number, otherwise None.

methods.name: soda.contracts.impl.yaml_helper.YamlHelper.read_number_opt
documentation:
An appropriate error log is generated if the value is not a number.
\:return: a Number if the value for the key is a YAML number, otherwise None.

methods.name: soda.contracts.check.CheckResult.get_contract_result_str_lines
documentation:
Provides the summary for the contract result logs, as well as the __str__ impl of this check result.
Method implementations can use self._get_outcome_line(self)

classes.name: soda.contracts.check.Threshold
documentation:
The threshold is exceeded when any of the member field conditions is True.
To be interpreted as a check fails when the metric value is ...greater_than or ...less_than etc...

classes.name: soda.contracts.check.Range
documentation: Boundary values are inclusive
